my_thoughts: The redefinition works: measurement criterion (speed = density, not linear time), defines: speed as density (phenomenological measure), provides: phenomenological measure. The student asks measurement question (seeking objective measurement), which shows they understand: speed = needs measurement (quantification). They'll likely test temporal definition next. Hypothesis: measurement redefinitions trigger temporal tests—testable by tracking whether measurement redefinitions lead to temporal inquiries.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student asks measurement question ('I do not understand about the speed of emotions. How can it be measured?')—seeking objective measurement via asks for criterion and seeks quantification. They understand: speed = needs measurement (quantification). The comparison_contrast mechanism redefines: measurement criterion (speed = density, not linear time), defines: speed as density (phenomenological measure), provides: phenomenological measure. Pattern: measurement question → redefinition → [next: temporal test]. Low confidence because redefinitions can lead to deeper questions or temporal tests.
